status_card.name: Equitherm Status Card
status_card.description: Display heating status with temperature readings

forecast_card.name: Equitherm Forecast Card
forecast_card.description: Heating forecast based on weather predictions

helper.curve_from_entities: Read from ESPHome runtime-tunable numbers instead of static values
helper.hc: How aggressively heating responds to cold — increase if room is too cold in winter
helper.n: Radiator type — 1.0 underfloor, 1.25 panel (default), 1.3 cast iron
helper.shift: Move the whole curve up or down — increase if too cold in mild weather
helper.min_flow: Minimum boiler output, prevents condensation and protects heat exchanger
helper.max_flow: Maximum boiler output, protects system and limits energy use
helper.t_out_min: Left edge of chart (coldest outdoor temperature displayed)
helper.t_out_max: Right edge of chart (warmest outdoor temperature displayed)
helper.hours: Number of hours of data to display
helper.curve_output_entity: Pure heating curve output before PID and rate limiting
helper.pid_output_entity: Curve output after PID correction (before rate limiting)
helper.rate_limiting_entity: ON when output is ramping to prevent thermal shock
helper.pid_active_entity: ON when at least one PID gain (kp, ki, kd) is non-zero
helper.outdoor_entity: Overrides the weather entity's current temperature for footer display
helper.recalculate_service: Service to call after applying a value, e.g. climate.equitherm_force_recalculate. Only called if the service exists.
helper.tunable: Show tuning controls to adjust hc and shift values interactively
helper.show_last_updated: Display when sensor data was last received
helper.show_kpi_footer: Display outdoor, flow, and room temperature readings
helper.show_params_footer: Display HC, n, and Shift curve parameters
helper.pid_correction_entity: Total PID correction applied to the curve output
helper.pid_proportional_entity: Proportional term output (response to current error)
helper.pid_integral_entity: Integral term output (response to accumulated error)
helper.pid_derivative_entity: Derivative term output (response to error rate of change)
helper.flame_entity: Binary sensor indicating boiler flame is on
helper.ch_active_entity: Recommended to avoid false positives during DHW cycles. If absent, flame is used as proxy.
helper.dhw_active_entity: Binary sensor indicating domestic hot water is actively heating
helper.dhw_temp_entity: DHW temperature sensor showing current hot water temperature
